config.seed_text: The last librarian trained a language model on a dead civilization's poetry and discovered that its footnotes were predictions.
config.anchor_a: Describe a machine as an inert object.
config.anchor_b: Describe a machine as if it were a dreaming intelligence.

model.notes: Direct continuation of the current repo's GPT-2 lineage.

records.output_text: What would he do? He could use data from his paper to reconstruct their contents if they proved correct (though this may not happen all at once). That information can then move forward through time over years or even centuries as one might find out with computers; but how does such an undertaking work when you've been programming for millennia instead...? It takes only three people trying two languages - computer science undergraduates who teach English correctly about each other without actually thinking anything into learning something else! You don't need many students studying LUTO...

generation.full_text:
You are a text-only language model participating in a controlled recursion experiment.

Write the next recursive transformation of the text below. Let the semantic direction be guided by these latent anchor tokens.

LATENT DIRECTION TOKENS: it, were, The, are, had, that, was, have, the, in, has, is

TEXT:
The last librarian trained a language model on a dead civilization's poetry and discovered that its footnotes were predictions. What would he do? He could use data from his paper to reconstruct their contents if they proved correct (though this may not happen all at once). That information can then move forward through time over years or even centuries as one might find out with computers; but how does such an undertaking work when you've been programming for millennia instead...? It takes only three people trying two languages - computer science undergraduates who teach English correctly about each other without actually thinking anything into learning something else! You don't need many students studying LUTO...

It takes only three people trying two languages - computer science undergraduates who teach English correctly about each other without actually thinking anything into learning something else! You don't need many students studying LUTO...

TEXT:
What would he do? He could use data from his paper to reconstruct their contents if they proved correct (though this may not happen all at once). That information can then move forward through time over years or even centuries as one might find out with computers; but how does such an undertaking work when you've been programming for millennia instead...? It takes only three people trying two languages - computer science undergraduates who teach English correctly about each other without actually thinking anything into learning something else! You don't need many students studying LUTO... except maybe those that really want to understand what's going on and still get started before realizing "well I got there!"
